prompt:
Strip the kernel pin out of PR #530, leaving only the xdg portal restart policy.

## Context
PR #530 was scoped to add Restart=on-failure to xdg-desktop-portal user services (the actual purpose, in modules/common/desktop.nix). The agent that wrote it ALSO added an unrelated kernel pin to `hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix`:
```nix
boot.kernelPackages = lib.mkOverride 60 pkgs.linuxPackages_6_18;
```
The agent did this to fix the trunk-red ZFS-vs-zen-kernel CI failure. We're solving that problem differently in PR #531 (`zfs.broken = \"warn\"` override, preserves the zen kernel). #530 should not also pin the kernel — it would compete with #531 and would change the kernel away from zen, losing gaming optimizations.

## Steps
1. Check out the PR branch (`agent/20260509-1025-5b02`).
2. Revert the changes to `hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix` entirely. The kernel pin and its accompanying comment block (lines around the `boot.supportedFilesystems = [ \"zfs\" ]` declaration that #530 added before/after) should be removed. Confirm `git diff origin/main -- hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix` is empty after the revert.
3. Leave `modules/common/desktop.nix` alone — that's the actual content of this PR.
4. Verify the diff: only `modules/common/desktop.nix` should be changed. If anything else (e.g. `flake.lock`) was incidentally touched, leave it as-is unless it's clearly part of the kernel pin.
5. Commit with a clear message — something like "drop kernel pin; ZFS unblock handled by #531".
6. Force-push to the PR branch.
7. Update the PR description to remove any references to the kernel pin / ZFS unblock — this PR is portal restart only. Reference #531 as 'companion ZFS unblock landing separately.'

## CRITICAL: no local nix tooling
- No `nixos-rebuild`, no `nix build`, no `nix flake check`, no `nix eval`. PR #529 (OS-level memory protection) hasn't merged yet, so the running system isn't protected against build-induced wedges. CI does dry-run validation on a runner; that's the gate.
- After the strip, CI will still fail on classic-laddie because trunk is red on ZFS — that's expected and is fixed by #531 landing first. Don't try to make CI green from this PR.

## Acceptance
- `hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix` matches main (no diff)
- `modules/common/desktop.nix` retains the portal restart policy
- PR description scoped to portal restart only
- Force-pushed to existing branch
- (CI on classic-laddie will still fail until #531 merges; ignore)
